# Source Data for "Decoding cell signaling via optimal transport and information theory"

This folder contains the numerical input data and processed output data underlying the main and supplementary figures. It is organized for Nature Communications source-data review. The index file `Source_Data_Index.xlsx` provides the figure-level map, folder descriptions, file manifest, and column descriptions in Excel format.

## Folder structure

* `Raw_Input_Data/`: numerical input data used by the analysis scripts.
* `Processed_Output_Data/`: numerical outputs generated by the analysis scripts and used to plot the main and supplementary figures.
* `Source_Data_Index.xlsx`: Excel workbook containing the figure-level source-data map, folder index, file manifest, and column descriptions.

## Raw input data descriptions

### TNF data

The TNF signaling input data were extracted from the supplementary figures of the following study:

Cheong R, Rhee A, Wang CJ, Nemenman I, Levchenko A. Information transduction capacity of noisy biochemical signaling networks. Science. 2011;334:354–358. doi:10.1126/science.1204553.

The present analysis uses TNF dose-dependent NF-κB and ATF-2 response statistics for WT and A20-deficient cells at 30 min and 4 hr after TNF stimulation. These values were extracted from the supplementary figures of Cheong et al. and organized into text files for the present optimal-transport/information-theoretic reanalysis.

Specifically, the mutual information values used as target values were extracted from Supplementary Fig. 3B of Cheong et al. For the present analysis, only the single-pathway information values were used, corresponding to the first two response cases: NF-κB only and ATF-2 only. The combined NF-κB and ATF-2 information values were not used for the present one-input/one-output analysis.

The dose-dependent mean and standard deviation values were extracted from Supplementary Fig. 10 of Cheong et al. These statistics were used to construct Gaussian conditional response distributions for each TNF dose, cell type, response pathway, and time point.

The TNF input files are organized as follows.

#### 30 min after TNF stimulation

`raw_input_data/TNF/30min/` contains:

```text
X-meanYcX-stdYcX-WT-30min-nfkb.txt
X-meanYcX-stdYcX-WT-30min-atf-2.txt
X-meanYcX-stdYcX-A20-30min-nfkb.txt
X-meanYcX-stdYcX-A20-30min-atf-2.txt
```

#### 4 hr after TNF stimulation

`raw_input_data/TNF/4hr/` contains:

```text
X-meanYcX-stdYcX-WT-4hr-nfkb.txt
X-meanYcX-stdYcX-WT-4hr-atf-2.txt
X-meanYcX-stdYcX-A20-4hr-nfkb.txt
X-meanYcX-stdYcX-A20-4hr-atf-2.txt
```

Each `X-meanYcX-stdYcX-*.txt` file contains three columns:

```text
TNF_dose_ng_per_mL    conditional_mean_response_a.u.    conditional_standard_deviation_a.u.
```

#### Mutual information target values

`raw_input_data/TNF/target_MI/` contains:

```text
MI-nfkb.txt
MI-atf-2.txt
```

The `MI-nfkb.txt` file contains the extracted TNF-to-NF-κB mutual information values for WT and A20-deficient cells at 30 min and 4 hr. The `MI-atf-2.txt` file contains the corresponding TNF-to-ATF-2 mutual information values.

These extracted numerical values are used here only as input data for reproducing the present reanalysis. Users should cite the original Cheong et al. study when using these TNF response data. Small deviations may arise from digitization of the published supplementary figures.

### RAS-MAPK/SOS-RAF data

The SOS and RAF single-cell translocation data used for the RAS-MAPK reanalysis were obtained from the publicly available repository associated with the following study:

Umeki N, Kabashima Y, Sako Y. Evaluation of information flows in the RAS-MAPK system using transfer entropy measurements. eLife. 2025;14:e104432. doi:10.7554/eLife.104432.

Original repository:

```text
https://github.com/YasushiSako/transfer_entropy_2/
```

The original repository contains the data in the folder:

```text
EGF dose dynamics data/
```

For the present analysis, only the following four files were used:

```text
SOS_wt_DMSO_EGF10ng.csv
RAF_wt_DMSO_EGF10ng.csv
SOS_wt_10nMMEKi_EGF10ng.csv
RAF_wt_10nMMEKi_EGF10ng.csv
```

These files correspond to SOS and RAF single-cell translocation time courses after 10 ng/mL EGF stimulation under control/DMSO and 10 nM MEK-inhibited conditions. In each CSV file, the first column represents time after EGF stimulation in minutes, and the remaining columns represent single-cell signal intensities. SOS and RAF responses with the same cell index were measured simultaneously in the same cell, as described in the original repository.

The original repository states that these data are licensed under CC-BY-NC-SA 4.0. Therefore, the raw SOS/RAF CSV files are not redistributed here. Users should download the four files listed above directly from the original repository and cite the original study. This Source Data folder provides the processed output files generated from these input data for the present optimal-transport/information-theoretic reanalysis.

## Processed output data descriptions

### Theoretical data

`processed_output_data/Theoretical/` contains motif-level numerical outputs for informational fidelity, geometric fidelity, mutual information, 2-Wasserstein distance, optimized noise parameters, and related quantities. Files containing `lambda-W-I-rho` include values of the Lagrange multiplier, 2-Wasserstein distance, mutual information, and correlation. Files containing `W-I` contain 2-Wasserstein distance and mutual information values used for fidelity plots.

The `C1FFL/h-2/` folder contains the cooperative-binding C1-FFL check with Hill coefficient `h = 2`.

The numerical data for the illustrative unimodal-to-bimodal non-Gaussian relay example are included within:

```text
Processed_Output_Data/Theoretical/Gauss-nonGauss-Compare/
```

### TNF processed data

`Processed_Output_Data/TNF_30min/` and `processed_output_data/TNF_4hr/` contain numerical outputs generated from the TNF input files.

These folders include:

* `MI_WD_summary/`: mutual information and 2-Wasserstein distance values with uncertainty estimates.
* `conditional_marginal_joint_distributions/`: conditional distributions, input marginals, output marginals, and joint distributions.
* `cdf_quantile_functions/`: cumulative distribution functions and quantile-function data used for 2-Wasserstein distance calculation and plotting.
* `convergence/`: numerical convergence diagnostics.
* `mapping_factors_and_fit_curves/`: Hill/linear fit curves and mapping-factor estimates.

### RAS-MAPK processed data

`Processed_Output_Data/RAS_MAPK_SOS_RAF/` contains the processed numerical outputs used for the SOS-RAF/RAS-MAPK analysis. These include time series, marginal distributions, joint distributions, dynamic range, correlation, mean, standard deviation, mutual information, 2-Wasserstein distance, and inverse 2-Wasserstein distance.

Marginal-distribution files begin with:

```text
Marginal_dist_
```

Joint-distribution files begin with:

```text
Joint_dist_
```

In these files and scripts, `DMSO` denotes the control condition, and `Drug` or `10nMMEKi` denotes the MEK-inhibited condition.

## Notes

* `WT` denotes wild-type cells.
* `A20` or `A20-/-` denotes A20-deficient cells.
* `DMSO` denotes the control condition in the RAS-MAPK/SOS-RAF analysis.
* `Drug` or `10nMMEKi` denotes the MEK-inhibited condition in the RAS-MAPK/SOS-RAF analysis.
* `MI` denotes mutual information.
* `2-WD` denotes 2-Wasserstein distance.
* `W2_inverse`, `WDinv`, or inverse 2-WD denotes the geometric-fidelity measure used in the manuscript.
